verses.text: At Rephidim there is no water; the people chide Moses and tempt the LORD. Thirst. Again want turns to murmuring.
At Rephidim there is no water; the people chide Moses and tempt the LORD. Thirst. Again want turns to murmuring.
At Rephidim there is no water; the people chide Moses and tempt the LORD. Thirst. Again want turns to murmuring.
God bids Moses smite the rock in Horeb, and water flows; he names it Massah and Meribah. Rock smitten. From the struck rock comes life-giving water.
God bids Moses smite the rock in Horeb, and water flows; he names it Massah and Meribah. Rock smitten. From the struck rock comes life-giving water.
God bids Moses smite the rock in Horeb, and water flows; he names it Massah and Meribah. Rock smitten. From the struck rock comes life-giving water.
God bids Moses smite the rock in Horeb, and water flows; he names it Massah and Meribah. Rock smitten. From the struck rock comes life-giving water.
Amalek attacks; Moses sends Joshua to fight and stands on the hill with the rod. Battle. The first foe of the freed people rises.
Amalek attacks; Moses sends Joshua to fight and stands on the hill with the rod. Battle. The first foe of the freed people rises.
While Moses' hands are up, Israel prevails; Aaron and Hur hold them up till sunset. Held hands. Victory hangs on upheld prayer.
While Moses' hands are up, Israel prevails; Aaron and Hur hold them up till sunset. Held hands. Victory hangs on upheld prayer.
While Moses' hands are up, Israel prevails; Aaron and Hur hold them up till sunset. Held hands. Victory hangs on upheld prayer.
While Moses' hands are up, Israel prevails; Aaron and Hur hold them up till sunset. Held hands. Victory hangs on upheld prayer.
God swears to blot out Amalek; Moses builds an altar, 'The LORD my banner.' Jehovah-nissi. The Lord Himself is His people's standard.
